reasoning: The stock recently surged roughly 14% around its Q4 earnings report on February 3, 2026, propelling it from ~260 to a near-term high of 297.7 by February 13. While momentum is currently positive, this sharp jump makes the stock technically overbought compared to historical averages and standard trading bands. Statistical trend models incorporating recent data favor a mean reversion (estimated P(increase) around 0.39 or slightly higher accounting for momentum), as extreme post-earnings runs often cool down to consolidate gains. The ex-dividend record date isn't until March 31, meaning no mechanical $1.61 drop will hit the stock before the Mar 17 resolution. However, given the exceptionally high baseline price, the probability of closing strictly higher favors the downside (mean reversion). In keeping with the rule that longer horizons revert toward 0.5 due to elevated variance, the March probability is set closer to 50% than the February probability.
